methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Kinova Robotics is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $51.6M to date. The comparable universe for this sector includes one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical or adjacent stages with similar valuation tiers does not exist—only three same-stage comps were needed but zero were identified—limiting multiples-based approaches.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation method. Using total capital raised of $52M as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage progression, this method implies a valuation of $62M. This reflects the incremental value creation expected between funding rounds at this maturity level, absent extraordinary growth or market multiples compression.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M to $71M, centered on $62M. This range is derived from sensitivity around the 1.2x multiplier applied to the $52M raised base. Key uncertainties include: the absence of narrowly-comparable peers limits confidence in external benchmarking; revenue, burn rate, and margin profile are not provided; and current market conditions in robotics remain volatile. A more precise valuation would require operating metrics and access to recent late-stage fundraising data in this subsector.
